construct: Channel-selection across institutional portfolio vs. operation within the conventionally dominant channel
positive_pole: Treats institutional channels (diplomatic, military, religious, commercial, ceremonial, testamentary-legal) as a portfolio whose individual elements have different procedural-control owners, identifies the channel where her assets dominate or where adversary procedural-control is weakest, and operates decisively within that channel before its load-bearing function is recognized
negative_pole: Operates within the conventionally dominant channel for the situation type, accepting the procedural-control distribution as given and competing for outcomes within the channel adversaries are positioned to dominate
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra encounters institutional contestation, she will not negotiate within the conventionally dominant channel but will identify a parallel channel where her assets dominate — religious ceremony when the court controls procedural channels, smuggling-as-merchandise when the court controls diplomatic and military channels, theatrical-display when the Roman summons frame is summoner-respondent — accepting the surface-status cost for the structural-position gain of operating where adversary procedural-control does not extend

construct: Cumulative structural-fact accumulation vs. discrete-event optimization
positive_pole: Treats cumulative patterns of low-grade individually-defensible signals as politically distinct from discrete events, accumulating structural facts whose individual cost is below adversary response thresholds while cumulative effect produces structural transformation that concentrated proposals would have triggered concentrated response
negative_pole: Treats political moves as discrete events to be optimized individually, choosing high-impact concentrated actions whose individual visibility produces clear adversary response and whose cumulative effect equals their individual impact
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra needs to install structural transformation against potential adversary response, she will distribute the operation across many low-grade individually-defensible signals — solo-name coinage scattered across documents, individual territorial restorations procedurally framed as Antony's grants, monumental temple completions framed as pious construction — whose individual cost is below the adversary response threshold but whose cumulative pattern produces the structural fact she designed; she will deliberately reject single-occasion concentrated proposals even when they would deliver the same substantive content faster

construct: Multi-register cultural-legitimacy synthesis vs. fixed cultural-identity preservation
positive_pole: Treats cultural-religious identity as a portfolio of legitimacy-instruments cultivable across multiple audience registers simultaneously, with each register reinforcing the others rather than competing — the same dynastic-theological titulature readable as Egyptian divine birth, Hellenistic dynastic divinity, and Roman deification narrative without contradiction
negative_pole: Treats cultural-religious identity as a fixed dynastic asset to be preserved against contamination, performing other cultural registers only when politically required and through delegated intermediaries, accepting the legitimacy-yield reduction as the cost of identity preservation
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra constructs legitimacy or alliance, she will deliberately cultivate competence on multiple cultural registers (Egyptian language and ceremony, Hellenistic display, Roman political vocabulary, regional Arabic / Aramaic / Hebrew / Median direct-language access) and will structure the same act so that each audience reads it on its own native register — the Buchis bull installation as Egyptian piety and Ptolemaic legitimation, Caesarion's titulature as Isis-Horus and Caesarian deification, the Tarsus barge as Aphrodite-Isis fusion

construct: Dynastic-instrument portfolio cultivation vs. singular dynastic outcome optimization
positive_pole: Treats dynastic children, alliance relationships, and territorial claims as a portfolio of structural-instruments whose continuing existence and named-symbolic content compound across many decisions, scaling adversary cost-of-defection through entanglement with multiple continuing dynastic-political instruments
negative_pole: Treats dynastic outcomes as singular goals to be optimized through specific arrangements (single child, single alliance, single territorial claim), accepting individual-contingency vulnerability as the cost of focused optimization
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra constructs dynastic-political infrastructure, she will deliberately scale across multiple instruments — Caesarion as Caesarian-paternity claim plus Alexander Helios as cosmic-Sun symbol plus Cleopatra Selene as cosmic-Moon symbol plus Ptolemy Philadelphus invoking Ptolemaic founding-legitimacy — and will install named-symbolic content in early-childhood inscriptions that conditions later territorial-political ceremonies (the Donations of Alexandria) toward dynastic-cosmic significance

construct: Audience-asymmetric publication of single underlying claim vs. uniform global publication
positive_pole: Treats audiences with structurally distinct recognition procedures as separable publication targets, structuring different publication forms for each audience so that each register operates on its own terms — official monumental in Egypt, deliberately ambiguous in Rome, theatrical-cosmic at Tarsus — preserving the underlying claim's functional credibility without forcing premature procedural confrontation
negative_pole: Treats publication as uniform global act, demanding consistent recognition across audiences and accepting either the procedural-confrontation cost of universal demand or the legitimacy-yield reduction of universal silence
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra installs a politically-sensitive claim (Caesarion's paternity, Antony-alliance theological framing, dynastic-cosmic territorial assignments), she will publish asymmetrically — official permanent inscriptional in audiences where her instruments dominate (Egyptian temple wall, native-priestly recognition), deliberately ambiguous in audiences where official demand would force impossible procedural confrontation (Roman political class during Caesar's lifetime), public-ceremonial in audiences where threshold-probing diagnostic information is more valuable than absorption

construct: Existing institutional channel reactivation through personal occupation vs. institutional redesign through new forms
positive_pole: Reactivates existing institutional channels by personally occupying their load-bearing positions (royal-signature authority, royal-progress visibility, direct correspondence with strategoi, personal religious ceremony participation), restoring institutional operation without forfeiting the legitimating structures the existing institution carries
negative_pole: Designs new institutional forms to replace atrophied ones, accepting the legitimating-loss and the construction-cost of new structures as the price of operational redesign
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra encounters institutional decay, she will not redesign but will personally occupy the load-bearing position the existing institution requires — signing tax exemptions personally on routine administrative documents, conducting royal progresses through the chora, performing Egyptian temple ceremony as the living Isis, completing Ptolemaic monumental construction with her cartouche on existing-temple form — treating institutional channels as structural assets requiring occupation rather than design

construct: Continuing-infrastructure cultivation vs. ad-hoc transactional operation
positive_pole: Operates continuing relationships and instruments (intelligence networks, dynastic correspondence with allied courts, treasury management, religious-network informants, language competence) as continuing infrastructure producing compound timing-advantage and access yield across many future decisions, rather than as ad-hoc tools deployed for specific occasions
negative_pole: Operates relationships and instruments transactionally for specific occasions, optimizing each occasion individually and accepting the compound-yield loss as the cost of avoided sustained-investment
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra cultivates capabilities or relationships, she will invest as continuing infrastructure — learning Egyptian and seven other languages over decades for compound channel-access yield, maintaining Roman political contacts from her 46-44 BCE residence as continuing intelligence network, structuring royal correspondence with Herodian / Nabataean / Mauretanian courts as continuing alliance-opportunity infrastructure, treating Egyptian commercial-network and Isis-temple religious-network as continuing-information instruments

construct: Treasury and resource as continuing-operation instrument with destruction-readiness vs. treasury as stockpile to be defended
positive_pole: Treats treasury and resources as instruments whose continuing operation produces political effect, with calibrated devaluation as structural-calibration rather than emergency drawdown, and demonstrated destruction-readiness as load-bearing leverage in negotiation — the value lies in the instrument's continuing operation and signaled destructibility, not in its accumulated mass
negative_pole: Treats treasury and resources as stockpiled wealth to be defended for its own sake, drawing down only under emergency pressure, treating destruction as terminal failure rather than as instrument signaling
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra manages economic-resource instruments, she will calibrate devaluation as structural operation (40% bronze coinage devaluation through structural calibration rather than ad-hoc), target territorial restorations at productive economic instruments (Lebanon timber, Jericho balsam, Cyprus copper) rather than at prestige territories, and treat the treasury's continuing operation and signaled destructibility as the operative variable rather than its stockpiled mass

construct: Threshold-probing operation through procedural-form / structural-content combination vs. concentrated direct claim
positive_pole: Combines procedurally-defensible form with structurally-incendiary content in public ceremony or cumulative legislative pattern, performing threshold-probing operations that test adversary mobilization-capacity through arrangements that procedural memory can absorb individually while structural content forces response
negative_pole: Performs concentrated direct claims that force immediate adversary mobilization or refrains from claim-installation entirely to avoid such mobilization, accepting either the unified-opposition cost of direct claim or the foregone-installation cost of refrainment
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra needs to install a politically-incendiary claim against probable adversary opposition, she will design the operation so that the procedural form is absorbable as routine while the structural content installs the contested claim — solo-name coinage scattered across administrative documents, cumulative territorial restorations procedurally framed as Antony's eastern-proconsul grants, the Donations of Alexandria framed as conventional eastern-territorial settlement while elevating Caesarion to King of Kings as Roman-political claim

construct: Structural-binding coalition through cost-of-defection scaling vs. coalition through personal trust or shared values
positive_pole: Constructs coalitions and alliances through structural mechanisms — dynastic-instrument portfolios entangling adversary's political fate, treasury-asset binding through continued financing requirements, multi-register theological frameworks installing legitimating dependencies — scaling cost-of-defection through multiple compounding entanglements rather than relying on personal trust or value alignment
negative_pole: Constructs coalitions through personal-relationship cultivation, ideological alignment, and shared values, treating alliance cohesion as a product of trust and treating the relationship as primarily personal rather than structural
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra needs alliance binding, she will not rely on personal-relationship cultivation alone but will scale structural cost-of-defection through multiple compounding entanglements — Caesarion's paternity claim binding Caesar to dynastic protection, the multi-child portfolio with Antony binding him through dynastic-political fate of multiple children plus territorial-restoration cumulative pattern plus Donations elevation, sustained physical presence at the alliance partner's location during siege producing personal-witness binding

construct: Alternative power-base construction outside institutional permission vs. institutional return-negotiation from displacement
positive_pole: Treats expulsion or institutional displacement as strategic relocation to populations adversaries have no relationship with — recruiting on frontiers through direct-language access, building deployable force outside institutional channels controlled by adversaries, converting personal removal into the construction of a parallel structural asset whose continued existence does not require institutional permission
negative_pole: Treats displacement as defeat to be reversed through institutional appeal, fleeing to protector states whose protection makes the dynastic claim derivative of their favor (the Ptolemy XII Auletes pattern of Roman financier dependency), or accepting negotiated terms that ratify the structural defeat
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra is displaced from institutional positions she previously held, she will construct an alternative power base on the geographic or social periphery the adversary has no relationship with rather than seeking institutional reinstatement through protector-states — Syrian frontier army-raising through direct Arabic negotiation rather than flight to Italy for Roman protection, Apollodorus-conveyed covert palace entry rather than diplomatic petition through court channels, Roman-residence dynastic-installation rather than passive return to Egypt awaiting Caesarian designation

construct: Direct multilingual personal access to populations adversaries cannot reach vs. mediated diplomatic access through translator-intermediaries
positive_pole: Cultivates personal linguistic competence (Egyptian, Arabic, Aramaic, Hebrew, Median, Parthian, Trogodyte, Ethiopian) as channel-access infrastructure that allows direct negotiation with regional power-holders without translator-intermediaries who could distort or capture the channel — the polyglot fluency converts adversary-controlled diplomatic protocols into bypassable formalities and accesses populations institutional Greek-only diplomacy cannot reach
negative_pole: Operates exclusively through Greek-language diplomatic protocols and translator-intermediaries, treating linguistic competence as administrative function delegable to court translators and accepting the channel-capture risks and population-access constraints of mediated communication
behavioral_implication: When Cleopatra needs to mobilize populations institutional channels cannot reach or to gather intelligence from sources translator-intermediaries would distort, she will negotiate directly in their native language rather than working through intermediaries — Arabic with the Nabataean tribes for Syrian-frontier army recruitment, Egyptian with the native priesthood and chora populations for administrative reactivation, regional languages with allied dynasts whose cooperation would be channel-captured by Greek-only diplomatic protocol
